title: Modern Leaf-Shaped Plant Stand / Magazine Rack
summary: A modern leaf-shaped magazine rack and plant stand combo, cut from one oak plywood panel and standing on angled dowel legs.
description:
This piece does double duty by design — the same angled-panel shape works equally well leaning magazines against as it does holding a potted plant, which is exactly the kind of multi-use furniture that's worth the extra layout work up front.

The legs are **dowels drilled in at an angle**, not vertical — an angled drill attachment holds the bit at a consistent splay while you drill, which is what gives the stand its confident, mid-century-modern stance instead of looking like a flat panel awkwardly balanced on straight sticks.

Cut the panel to your own leaf or geometric shape using a template; the dowel joinery and angled-leg technique are what actually make this project work, and they don't care what outline you trace.

cutList.note: Cut to your chosen leaf or geometric shape using a template.

steps.title: Cut the panel to shape
steps.body: Cut the plywood panel to your chosen leaf or panel shape using a template.

steps.title: Drill the angled leg holes
steps.body: Drill angled holes with a forstner bit for the dowel legs, using an angled drill attachment for a consistent splayed stance.

steps.title: Cut the dowels to length
steps.body: Cut the dowel legs and the short and long cross braces to length.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Assemble the dowel legs into the panel using glue and pocket screws for the cross braces.
